title: Distributed Data Parallel and FSDP from Scratch

questions.question: Which two collective ops are the core of a from-scratch DDP wrapper?
options: broadcast at construction so every rank starts from the same parameters, and all_reduce after backward so every rank ends with the averaged gradient
questions.explanation: Broadcast at init keeps ranks in sync at step zero. All-reduce after backward keeps them in sync at every step that follows.

questions.question: Why does this lesson work on CPU without CUDA?
options: torch.distributed ships a gloo backend that runs collectives over plain sockets, so torch.multiprocessing workers on CPU form a real process group; only the device tag changes on a GPU box
It does not use torch.distributed at all
questions.explanation: Gloo is the CPU collective backend. The same call sites work on nccl on GPU; the API surface is identical.

questions.question: What does the manual_all_reduce_matches_single_process test prove?
options: That summing per-rank gradients and dividing by world_size recovers the gradient a single process would compute on the concatenated input, within floating point noise
questions.explanation: The cross entropy mean reduction on the full batch equals the average of per-rank means. Sum-and-divide is the right operation; the test makes that visible.

questions.question: What does the FSDP sketch in this lesson do per parameter on every forward pass?
options: Pads the flat parameter to a multiple of world_size, slices, all_gathers the slices on every rank, drops the pad, and verifies the reconstruction matches the original
questions.explanation: Each rank owns 1/world_size of every parameter. The gather rebuilds the full tensor for compute; production FSDP overlaps the gather with the previous layer's work.

questions.question: Why is the gloo all_gather sized to world_size equal-size shards even when the parameter length is not divisible by world_size?
options: Gloo's all_gather requires equal output tensor sizes per rank, so the flat parameter is right-padded to a multiple of world_size before slicing and the padding is dropped after the gather
questions.explanation: Gloo (and nccl) all_gather expects same-size outputs. Padding is the cheapest fix; the sketch trims the result back to the original length.

questions.question: Reading the demo output, param_sum_spread near zero across ranks tells you what?
options: That every rank ended the run at the same parameter values, which means the broadcast at construction and the all-reduce after every backward both worked; if either was broken the spread would grow over steps
questions.explanation: DDP's invariant is parameter parity across ranks. The spread is the audit; the demo asserts it stays under 1e-3.
